identity.title: Other Similar Organizations (except Business, Professional, Labor, and Political Organizations)

lens.included_activities: U.S. lower-middle-market organizations in NAICS 813990 that repeatedly provide member-interest, governance, administration, communications, regulatory, event, or community-association services funded by recurring dues, assessments, fees, sponsorships, or repeat programs, including property-owner, condominium, homeowner, cooperative-owner, tenant, and regulatory athletic associations.
lens.excluded_activities: Religious, grantmaking, social-advocacy, civic and social, business, professional, labor, and political organizations classified elsewhere; recreational clubs and teams that primarily operate activities rather than regulate or administer a league; volunteer-only bodies without transferable operations; shells, captive units, non-control financing vehicles, and organizations outside the target normalized-EBITDA band.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Members and represented communities pay recurring dues, assessments, initiation charges, program or certification fees, and event charges; some organizations also earn sponsorship, advertising, licensing, disciplinary, management, or service revenue. Staff deliver governance support, member records, billing and collections, communications, meetings, compliance, vendor coordination, events, and issue resolution.

a.rationale: Member databases, dues and assessment billing, inquiries, notices, meeting materials, minutes, rule lookup, document drafting, vendor comparisons, event administration, reporting, and routine communications are exposed to AI assistance. Board relations, negotiations, sensitive enforcement, live events, inspections, facility work, and accountable decisions remain human-intensive.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted task and time study of 813990 organizations split between community associations and athletic or regulatory associations, with current association-management software separated from new AI capability, would materially change the range.
caveats: BLS publishes the occupation mix for broad NAICS 813900, not exact 813990, and that parent includes business, professional, labor, and political organizations excluded from this code's title.
The proxy shows a knowledge- and administration-heavy parent occupation mix but does not measure task-level AI exposure or current automation.
Community associations may outsource physical property work while athletic and regulatory associations may devote more staff time to events, relationships, and adjudication.
a.bridge: Source population/quantity: the official 813990 member-interest and membership-service definitions plus May 2023 OEWS employment and wages for broad NAICS 813900, where management was 19.84%, business and financial operations 28.07%, office and administrative support 20.84%, computer and mathematical 3.45%, arts/media 5.56%, legal 1.01%, and sales 2.08% of employment. Transformation: mapped portions of those groups' records, billing, analysis, drafting, communications, research, scheduling, and support tasks to exposed hours, with low exposure assigned to governance judgment, negotiations, live events, inspections, and physical work. Error direction: the broader parent may overstate exposure if exact 813990 contains more field, facility, event, or stakeholder work, while unmeasured administrative work in small associations may understate it.

rho.rationale: Five years permits AI-assisted member service, document search and drafting, meeting preparation, collections triage, vendor analysis, communications, and event workflows. Realization is constrained by volunteer boards, fragmented association-management systems, small staffs, sensitive resident or member disputes, records quality, privacy, legal review, change budgets, and the need for accountable human decisions.
rho.change_evidence: Pre/post deployment data from community-association managers and athletic or regulatory associations covering staff hours, response time, collection rates, errors, dispute escalation, legal cost, member satisfaction, and implementation expense would materially change the estimate.
caveats: No representative source measures AI implementation or labor realization in 813990.
Small volunteer-led organizations may lack clean digital records and implementation capacity.
Faster communications and analysis may raise service levels rather than reduce staffing.

e.rationale: Annual dues, assessments, member records, governance calendars, and repeat administration make many organizations operationally recurring. Eligibility is reduced because many are member-governed nonprofits or volunteer bodies without conventional normalized EBITDA, transferable equity, independent customer contracts, or management depth, while the frozen firm count is margin-bridged from a for-profit services margin across unlike entities.
e.change_evidence: A firm-level file linking 813990 establishments to legal form, tax status, recurring revenue, paid employment, outsourced management, normalized surplus or EBITDA, member concentration, ownership rights, and transferable contracts would materially change the estimate.
caveats: The frozen n estimate applies a business-services EBITDA margin to a category containing many nonprofit and member-governed entities.
Association operating scale and recurring assessments do not by themselves create transferable equity or normalized EBITDA.
Property-owner associations and athletic or regulatory leagues have materially different governance and revenue structures.

s5.rationale: A small subset of eligible organizations may transfer control through the sale of a for-profit management company, contract portfolio, or operating platform. Most 813990 entities are governed through member elections, bylaws, affiliations, or nonprofit boards rather than saleable ownership, so ordinary owner-sale intentions require a steep discount.
s5.change_evidence: Closed transaction counts matched to eligible 813990 entities from association-management brokers, state nonprofit merger filings, Form 990 organization records, and control changes in property and athletic association service platforms over a five-year cohort would materially change the estimate.
caveats: Gallup covers employer-business owners across industries and measures intentions, not completed transfers or member-governed organizations.
Board elections, nonprofit mergers, affiliation changes, and management-contract rebids are not necessarily qualifying control transfers.
Transactions may occur in separately classified association-management vendors rather than the 813990 organization itself.
s5.bridge: Source population/quantity: Gallup's fall-2024 survey found that 22% of employer-business owners planned to sell or transfer ownership within five years, while Census defines 813990 as organizations promoting members' interests. Transformation: treated 22% as a remote upper anchor and discounted sharply for nonprofit or member governance, absence of saleable equity, noncompletion, internal succession, contract rebids, and transactions occurring in vendors outside the code. Error direction: the broad owner survey substantially overstates qualifying 813990 control sales, while unobserved sales of for-profit operating platforms could make the low case conservative.

q.rationale: Annual dues, assessments, sponsorships, and program budgets can preserve implemented savings between budget cycles, while productivity may support more members or services without immediate repricing. Retention is limited by member-governed budgets, cost-based assessments, competitive management contracts, nonprofit missions, dues resistance, and pressure to reinvest savings in service, reserves, compliance, or events.
q.change_evidence: Matched budgets, dues and assessment schedules, management contracts, renewal decisions, service levels, staffing, reserves, and operating surplus before and after workflow automation across association types would materially change the estimate.
caveats: No representative evidence measures automation-benefit retention in 813990.
Member-owned associations may pass savings through lower assessments or improved services rather than retain profit.
Management contracts, sponsorships, dues, and regulatory fees have different repricing and pass-through behavior.

d5.rationale: Property and member governance, recurring assessments, communications, compliance, and organized athletic regulation should persist, but formation, consolidation, housing activity, participation, volunteer capacity, and member willingness to pay can shift demand. Broad industry employment is projected to be nearly flat over the decade.
d5.change_evidence: A five-year constant-price series of active members, association units, assessments, paid service hours, events, and regulatory transactions for exact 813990, split by community and athletic or regulatory associations, would materially change the estimate.
caveats: BLS projects employment rather than constant-price service demand and publishes only broad 813900, not exact 813990.
The 813900 projection includes business, professional, labor, and political organizations outside the exact code.
Housing-linked community associations and athletic or regulatory associations may follow different demand paths.
d5.bridge: Source population/quantity: BLS projects broad 813900 employment from 483,200 in 2024 to 488,000 in 2034, a 1.0% ten-year increase, while Census defines the exact member-interest service basket. Transformation: treated near-flat parent employment as a directional five-year demand anchor, centered the range at current quantity, and widened it for productivity, dues, participation, housing, and mix effects. Error direction: employment can understate demand when productivity rises and the broad parent may move differently from 813990 in either direction.

o.rationale: Member-interest organizations still need accountable governance, authority over rules and money, dispute handling, fiduciary controls, vendor oversight, live meetings or events, and responsibility to members. Self-service portals, automated communications, outsourced platforms, volunteer administration, and consolidation can remove some paid operator quantity without eliminating the underlying organization.
o.change_evidence: Member-cohort data comparing staffed organizations, volunteer self-administration, third-party management platforms, and software-first models on cost, service hours, compliance, disputes, collections, participation, and member retention would materially change the estimate.
caveats: The official definitions establish the service and revenue relationship but do not measure operator requirement.
An organization may remain legally necessary while most administration moves to volunteers, software, or an outside manager classified elsewhere.
Physical community operations and live athletic events are less replaceable than records, notices, dues collection, and routine member support.

narrative.executive_view: Other similar organizations combine recurring member relationships, dues or assessments, and substantial administrative workflows with unusually weak conventional ownership transferability. AI can improve records, communications, billing, meeting, and compliance work, but member governance and category heterogeneity limit clean roll-up comparability.
narrative.ai_impact: AI can assist member inquiries, document and rule search, notices, minutes, billing and collections, vendor comparisons, communications, reporting, and event administration. Boards, fiduciary decisions, disputes, negotiations, inspections, live events, and accountable rule enforcement remain human-led.
narrative.value_capture: Savings can remain inside annual dues, assessments, sponsorships, or program budgets and can support more service per staff member. Member-controlled budgets, cost-based assessments, management-contract competition, nonprofit missions, and reinvestment into reserves or service constrain retained benefit.
narrative.firm_availability: The frozen pool is highly uncertain because a for-profit margin bridge is applied to property, athletic, regulatory, nonprofit, and member-governed organizations. Repeated service does not ensure saleable equity, transferable contracts, normalized EBITDA, or a qualifying control path.
narrative.demand_durability: Member governance, community administration, recurring assessments, and athletic regulation are persistent needs, while broad industry employment is projected to be nearly flat. Demand varies with housing formation, participation, consolidation, volunteer capacity, regulation, and dues tolerance.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Key risks are nonprofit or member ownership, nontransferable governance, unreliable EBITDA classification, volunteer dependence, small staffs, fragmented systems, fiduciary and legal duties, member conflict, category mix, and transactions occurring in outside management vendors rather than the organization itself.
narrative.principal_driver: The principal driver is the recurring member-service relationship paired with AI-exposed records, billing, communications, meetings, and compliance administration.
narrative.principal_weakness: The principal weakness is that many establishments are member-governed organizations without conventional saleable ownership or a qualifying control-transfer mechanism.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Definition: 813990 Other Similar Organizations (except Business, Professional, Labor, and Political Organizations)
sources.supports: Official scope, member-interest purpose, illustrative association types, exclusions, lens heterogeneity, a, e, s5, q, d5, and o

sources.title: May 2023 OEWS: NAICS 813900 Business, Professional, Labor, Political, and Similar Organizations
sources.supports: Broad-parent occupation mix and wages, including management, business and financial, office support, computer, media, legal, and sales groups; a and rho

sources.title: BLS Employment and Output by Industry, 2024-2034
sources.supports: Broad 813900 employment of 483,200 in 2024 and 488,000 projected for 2034; d5

sources.title: Census NAPCS Product List for NAICS 813: Membership Services
sources.supports: Membership-service bundle offered for initiation fees and annual or periodic dues; revenue model, a, e, q, and o

sources.title: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.supports: Fall-2024 five-year sale or transfer intentions among employer-business owners; s5 proxy
